other_observers: With DFO trip including Tim Smart, Mike Wilson, Barb Nabors, Matt Newport, Doug Kibbe, Mackenzie Goldthwait, Debbie Barnes, Mary Ellen Moore, John Drummond, Betsy Shaw, Alison Kondler, Kelly Shipe, Genie Mitchell, Joe Brummer, Lori Brummer, Mark Mill; I was the original finder.
specific_location: Western edge of SW cove of Jumbo Reservoir approx. 40 deg 55.5 min N, 102 deg 40.2 min W
description: Medium-small shorebird with distinctive pigeon-like posture and overall warm buffy tones. Although distant, direct size comparisons with nearby Killdeer (smaller than), Baird's Sandpipers (similar in size but more upright), and unidentified peeps (bigger than) showed it to be a medium-small shorebird. Plumage detail in the back hard to discern at distance, but back looked contrastingly darker than the warm buffy underparts, neck, and face. Bill short and straight, eye looked relatively large on the roundish head, and the bird had a slender, long neck. Legs yellow and relatively long (longer, proportionately, than the peeps or Baird's, for example.)Behaviors: Stayed near water's edge but didn't enter the water like other shorebirds except Killdeer. Walked with an unusual (for a shorebird) pigeon-like gait, alternating between picking at the mud and picking at sticks, twigs, and branches draped with dried algae (and presumably insects.) All other shorebirds were probing underwater or picking from the muddy surface- none were picking from above ground level like this bird. Walked by many other shorebirds but didn't associate with them or move in tandem with any others.Call: not heardPlumage: likely juvenile
